info.name: Yahoo (formerly AOL) OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect API

params.name: client_id
params.description: Consumer Key issued when registering an app at developer.yahoo.com

params.name: redirect_uri
params.description: URI Yahoo will redirect to after the user authorizes the app

params.name: response_type
params.description: Must be `code` for the Authorization Code grant

params.name: scope
params.description: Space-separated OpenID Connect scopes (for example `openid profile email`)

params.name: state
params.description: Opaque value returned unchanged on the redirect; use to prevent CSRF

params.name: language
params.description: Language identifier for the Yahoo authorization UI

items.docs:
Redirects the user to Yahoo's authorization page where the user
authenticates and grants the requesting application access. On
success Yahoo redirects back to `redirect_uri` with a `code`
query parameter that can be exchanged at the token endpoint.

Exchanges an authorization code for access and refresh tokens, or
exchanges a refresh token for a new access token. The `grant_type`
parameter selects the behaviour. Client credentials may be passed
in the body or in an `Authorization: Basic` header with
base64-encoded `client_id:client_secret`.

Returns claims about the authenticated end user. The request must
carry a valid OAuth 2.0 access token issued with the `openid`
scope as a Bearer token.

Returns the JWKS used to verify Yahoo-issued ID tokens. Clients
should cache the keys and rotate based on `kid` values present in
the ID token header.
